_comment: Priority 6 / V2.0 addition. Config-driven enrichment metadata for EVERY detected Yoga/Dosha, keyed by the rule's exact 'name' (as emitted by yogaRuleEvaluator.js / doshaRuleEvaluator.js / advancedYogaRuleEvaluator.js / advancedDoshaRuleEvaluator.js). Consumed by services/rules/insightEnrichmentEvaluator.js to attach influence/severity/remedies/explanationMeta to each detected item WITHOUT changing its existing {name, detail} fields — purely additive, so /api/chart, /api/generate-report, and the frontend's existing consumption of chart.yogas/chart.doshas remain unaffected. If a detected item's name has no exact entry here (e.g. a dynamically-suffixed name like a specific Kaal Sarp Dosha subtype), insightEnrichmentEvaluator.js falls back to a safe generic default rather than failing.

Gaj Kesari Yoga.explanationMeta: A classic Raja-Yoga-adjacent combination linking Moon and Jupiter in mutual kendras; traditionally read as supporting intelligence, reputation, and resilience.

Budhaditya Yoga.explanationMeta: Sun-Mercury conjunction; traditionally associated with sharp intellect and communication skill, though it can be tempered if Mercury is combust.

Raj Yoga indication.explanationMeta: A general trikona-strength indicator for Jupiter; a loose, foundational signal rather than a formally named classical Raja Yoga.

remedies: Channeling Mars's energy through disciplined physical activity or leadership responsibility is traditionally suggested to make the best use of this yoga.
Ruchaka Yoga (Panch Mahapurusha).explanationMeta: One of the five Panch Mahapurusha Yogas; Mars strong in a kendra traditionally grants courage, leadership, and physical vitality.

remedies: Study, writing, or communication-based pursuits are traditionally recommended to make the best use of this yoga.
Bhadra Yoga (Panch Mahapurusha).explanationMeta: One of the five Panch Mahapurusha Yogas; Mercury strong in a kendra traditionally grants intellect, wit, and business acumen.

remedies: Devotional or teaching-oriented practice on Thursdays is traditionally suggested to support this yoga.
Hamsa Yoga (Panch Mahapurusha).explanationMeta: One of the five Panch Mahapurusha Yogas; Jupiter strong in a kendra traditionally grants wisdom, virtue, and respect.

remedies: Cultivating the arts, aesthetics, or relationship harmony is traditionally suggested to support this yoga.
Malavya Yoga (Panch Mahapurusha).explanationMeta: One of the five Panch Mahapurusha Yogas; Venus strong in a kendra traditionally grants comfort, charm, and refinement.

remedies: Disciplined, service-oriented routine on Saturdays is traditionally suggested to support this yoga.
Shasha Yoga (Panch Mahapurusha).explanationMeta: One of the five Panch Mahapurusha Yogas; Saturn strong in a kendra traditionally grants discipline, authority, and endurance.

Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga (Sun).explanationMeta: A debilitation-cancellation combination; traditionally read as turning an early-life weakness of the Sun into a later source of strength.

Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga (Moon).explanationMeta: A debilitation-cancellation combination; traditionally read as turning an early-life emotional weakness into later resilience.

Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga (Mars).explanationMeta: A debilitation-cancellation combination; traditionally read as converting Mars's low placement into eventual courage and drive.

Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga (Saturn).explanationMeta: A debilitation-cancellation combination; traditionally read as converting Saturn's low placement into eventual discipline and gain.

Viparita Raja Yoga.explanationMeta: An 'inverted' Raja Yoga where dusthana lords exchange dusthanas; traditionally read as unexpected gains arising after difficulty or reversal.

Chandra Mangal Yoga.explanationMeta: Moon-Mars conjunction; traditionally associated with financial drive and enterprise, but can also indicate emotional volatility if afflicted.

Lakshmi Yoga.explanationMeta: Venus strong in a kendra/trikona; traditionally associated with prosperity, grace, and comfort.

Saraswati Yoga.explanationMeta: Jupiter, Venus, and Mercury all well placed; traditionally associated with learning, eloquence, and artistic or scholarly success.

Adhi Yoga.explanationMeta: Natural benefics placed 6th/7th/8th from the Moon; traditionally associated with sustained success, leadership, and standing.

Sunapha Yoga.explanationMeta: A planet other than the Sun in the 2nd from the Moon; traditionally associated with self-made resourcefulness and earned wealth.

Anapha Yoga.explanationMeta: A planet other than the Sun in the 12th from the Moon; traditionally associated with personal comfort, health, and quiet dignity.

Durudhara Yoga.explanationMeta: Planets on both sides of the Moon (2nd and 12th); traditionally associated with wealth, vehicles, and support from others.

_parivartanaDefault.explanationMeta: Parivartana (mutual sign exchange) Yoga (basic support): the strength/nature of the two houses involved determines whether the exchange leans favorable (kendra/trikona lords) or more challenging (a dusthana house is involved); read together with each planet's own dignity and functional nature.

remedies: Reciting the Hanuman Chalisa and worshipping Hanuman on Tuesdays is a traditional remedy associated with Mangal Dosha.
Mangal Dosha.explanationMeta: Mars placed in a Manglik-sensitive house; traditionally considered relevant mainly to marital compatibility and timing, and is commonly said to be cancelled or reduced under several classical exceptions.

remedies: Performing a Rahu-Ketu shanti puja or Nag Panchami rituals is a traditional remedy associated with Kaal Sarp Dosha.
Kaal Sarp Dosha.explanationMeta: All seven classical planets hemmed between Rahu and Ketu; traditionally associated with delays, struggle, and karmic themes that often ease with age and effort.

remedies: Reciting the Hanuman Chalisa and worshipping Hanuman on Tuesdays is a traditional remedy associated with Manglik Dosha.
Manglik Dosha (Detailed).explanationMeta: A finer-grained severity classification of Mangal Dosha by house; severity is already carried on this item's own 'severity' field.

remedies: Performing Pitru Paksha rituals (Shraddha/Tarpan) for ancestors, and charity in their memory, is a traditional remedy associated with Pitru Dosha.
Pitru Dosha.explanationMeta: Sun afflicted by shadow planets, or Rahu/Ketu in the 9th house; traditionally linked to ancestral karmic debt and is addressed through ancestor-honoring rituals.

remedies: Worshipping Jupiter (Brihaspati) on Thursdays and avoiding hasty, unconventional decisions is a traditional remedy associated with Guru Chandal Yoga.
Guru Chandal Yoga.explanationMeta: Jupiter conjunct Rahu; traditionally read as wisdom and tradition being clouded by restlessness, though it can also indicate unconventional insight.

remedies: Worshipping Surya (Sun) and performing Rahu-shanti rituals is a traditional remedy associated with Grahan Yoga.
Grahan Yoga (Surya Grahan).explanationMeta: Sun conjunct Rahu/Ketu (an 'eclipse' configuration); traditionally associated with a clouded sense of self-confidence or authority.

remedies: Worshipping Chandra (Moon) and performing Rahu-Ketu shanti rituals is a traditional remedy associated with Grahan Yoga.
Grahan Yoga (Chandra Grahan).explanationMeta: Moon conjunct Rahu/Ketu (an 'eclipse' configuration); traditionally associated with clouded emotions or an unsettled mind.

remedies: Worshipping Shani (Saturn) on Saturdays and performing Rahu-Shani shanti rituals is a traditional remedy associated with Shrapit Yoga.
Shrapit Yoga.explanationMeta: Saturn conjunct Rahu; traditionally read as a sense of being 'cursed' or delayed until specific karmic lessons are resolved.

remedies: Worshipping Chandra (Moon) on Mondays and cultivating supportive community/relationships is a traditional remedy associated with Kemadruma Yoga.
Kemadruma Yoga.explanationMeta: No planet flanking the Moon; traditionally associated with a sense of isolation, though classical texts note it is cancelled by several common exceptions.
